checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-011 define observable routing decisions, fail-closed diagnostics, identity and manifest invariants, required report fields, cross-runtime equivalence, and release values; AC-001 through AC-007 and TEST-001 through TEST-006 provide concrete validation surfaces for each.

The goal's routing priority is covered by AC-001 and TEST-001, while bounded file-backed implementation and independent-review contexts are covered by AC-002 through AC-004 and TEST-002 through TEST-003.
AC-001 through AC-007 specify inspectable test outcomes, rejected tampering categories, required template fields, repository version state, and rollback behavior rather than subjective intent.

The Non-goals explicitly exclude globally pinned model IDs, reviewer/evaluator fallback, and chat-log task inputs; REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-005, and REQ-006 further bound role routing, substitutions, and host fallback behavior.

REQ-001 through REQ-007 explicitly constrain risk authority, deterministic processing, model availability and substitution, escalation and human reapproval, session isolation, fallback evidence, immutable snapshots, hashes, paths, identifiers, and cost provenance.

High-risk surfaces have planned validation: AC-001/TEST-001 cover policy-bound risk, escalation, unavailable tiers, deterministic-runtime failure, and terminal blocking; AC-002 through AC-004/TEST-002 through TEST-003 cover context reuse, manifest tampering, symlinks, traversal, mutable inputs, output roots, and provenance; AC-007/TEST-006 cover rollback atomicity and failure safety.
